кинул друг из biotech:

"single-cell RNA-seq solved biology"

lol no. we ran 40k cells/week and:
- batch effects ate signal unless you fight chemistry every run
- annotation is 70% manual curator time not compute
- "cell types" move when you change reference atlas (2023 vs 2024 Human Cell Atlas)
- cloud bill $12k/mo for pipeline that 2 postdocs could barely interpret

maybe the moat is reference data + QC SOPs not sequencing depth?

counter: "just scale to 10M cells" — ok but then you discover rare populations that are mostly doublets

not sure anyone outside lab cares but feels like everyone fundraising says "multi-omics platform play"
